signals.title: AUD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY trades at 112.772 as the G10 currency scorecard triggers a critical bilateral theme signal. Weekly performance shows AUD outperforming peers by +0.652% while JPY lags by -0.592%, generating a 1.244% spread. This divergence is structurally supported by a +335bp OIS carry premium (RBA AONIA 4.35% vs BoJ TONA 0.98%). Despite JPY net short positioning remaining highly crowded at -115,033 contracts (CFTC, week ending Jun 23), the carry advantage dominates in this normal volatility regime (VIX at 15.9). Watch for a break below 112.000 or a spike in VIX above 25 to trigger a rapid carry unwind.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY trades at 162.248, holding above the critical 160.00 level where BoJ intervention risk rises. The options market is actively hedging downside risk with a moderate put skew of -1.43 on the 25d risk reversal (Saxo, 1M tenor), while the volatility risk premium is elevated at +3.8% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.4%). This institutional caution contrasts sharply with retail sentiment, which is at an extreme 86% short, and CFTC speculative positioning, which remains heavily net short JPY at -115,033 contracts. The +268bp OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.66% vs TONA 0.98%) keeps the pair bid, but the options skew and intervention threat signal late-trend vulnerability. Watch the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul for policy action to trigger a squeeze.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6953 amid an extreme volatility risk premium of +30.0%, with 1M implied volatility at 37.1% vs a 30-day realized volatility of 7.1%. This massive premium indicates intense institutional hedging demand and options market fear, despite spot price action remaining relatively stable (+0.53% on the week). Speculators remain net long AUD at +29,035 contracts, supported by a -69bp OIS carry premium favoring AUD (SOFR 3.66% vs AONIA 4.35%). The extreme options pricing suggests the market is bracing for a major directional break. Watch the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug to resolve this volatility pricing anomaly.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + CESI Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4213 as its correlation with WTI Oil breaks to -0.876, deviating by -2.06σ from the 252-day norm of +0.014. This decoupling is driven by divergent macroeconomic data: the US Economic Surprise Index (CESI) is exceptionally strong at +282 net beats, while Canada's CESI lags at -6 net misses, reinforced by a weaker Q2 BOC Business Outlook Survey showing future sales indicators falling to +15 from +24. This fundamental divergence is backed by a +136bp OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.66% vs CORRA 2.30%) and a heavily crowded CAD net short position of -87,096 contracts. The correlation break signals that US-Canada growth and interest rate differentials have completely superseded oil price dynamics. Watch the next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 Jul to confirm this policy divergence.
evidence: US CESI vs CAD CESI: +282 vs -6

signals.title: USD/CHF — Swiss Franc Weakness + Real Rate Advantage
signals.text: USD/CHF trades at 0.8058, gaining 0.39% on the session following a Swiss unemployment surprise and broad USD strength. The pair is structurally supported by a massive +375bp OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.66% vs SARON -0.04%) and a highly restrictive US real policy rate of +1.49% compared to Switzerland's accommodative real rate of -0.60%. Speculators are net short CHF at -14,846 contracts, while the options market prices in an extreme volatility risk premium of +27.7% (IV 33.7% vs HV30 6.0%), indicating heavy demand for tail-risk protection. The combination of carry advantage and real rate divergence keeps USD/CHF structurally bid. Watch the next SNB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 24 Sep for any shift in their accommodative stance.
evidence: US vs CHF Real Rate: +1.49% vs -0.60%
